en: The scaffolding must be capable of withstanding the resulting work and traffic loads.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 58(2), 72(9)

Scaffold access routes must include secure ladders or stairs attached to scaffolds. Scaffold access placement must ensure work areas are within 20 m.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 58(7), 72(4)

Scaffolding inspections must be performed by qualified persons after erection, before first use, after weather or interruptions, monthly for system scaffolds, weekly for other scaffolds, and daily for suspended scaffolds.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 60(2), 61

Scaffold erection and dismantling responsibilities must involve trained and competent persons. Fit and competent persons must demonstrate physical, mental, and professional suitability.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 5(4)2, 60(6)
Qualified Personnel for Erection and Dismantling
Worker instruction content must address assembly safety, fall risks, loads, and hazards.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 60(6)
